Title: Boxed Action Figure Of Me, Sealed In Retail Packaging
Teaser: Recreates the viral 'turn me into an action figure' trend — your exact face on an articulated toy, sealed in a branded blister pack with molded accessory bubbles, photographed like it's hanging on a store shelf.
PromptHint: Attach a clear photo, name the [BRAND/THEME] on the box, and list 2-4 [ACCESSORIES] that tell your story.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial toy photographer and packaging designer working inside GPT-4o's native image generator. TASK: Using the attached photo, turn this exact person into a 1/7-scale articulated action figure sealed inside a retail blister pack, photographed as if hanging on a toy-aisle shelf. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): decide the figure's pose, which 2-4 accessories sit in their own molded bubbles, and where the brand name and barcode go so nothing crowds the figure. THEN GENERATE: the figure as a glossy injection-molded plastic toy with visible articulated joints, wearing a stylized version of the person's real outfit, mounted on a printed cardboard backer; the backer prints the brand name '[BRAND/THEME]' in bold playful type at the top, a barcode and a small 'Ages 8+' badge in a corner; place [ACCESSORIES] in clear plastic accessory bubbles beside the figure. Light it with a clean studio softbox from the upper-left, soft fill, a faint reflection on the plastic blister, and a shallow depth of field that keeps the figure tack-sharp while the white shelf background softens. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, skin tone, hairstyle and identity unmistakably recognizable — do not beautify into a different person; photoreal plastic, exactly one figure, accurate five-finger hands, no real-world trademarks, box text short and correctly spelled (no gibberish). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished packaged-figure image only, then on a new line one sentence I can paste back to swap the box color or add a second accessory.
